name: Fixture epic for 0.6.0 gate validation
description: Synthetic epic exercising the 0.6.0 gate requirements for phases 3, 6, 7, and 8.

parties.name: admin
parties.description: Internal staff who configure the system.

parties.name: user
parties.description: End user who interacts with features.

e2e.approach: Per-wave Chrome MCP validation only — no checked-in suite.

decisions.title: Use HTTP polling instead of GraphQL subscriptions
decisions.context: Real-time progress UX is needed; subscriptions add transport complexity disproportionate to the requirement.
decisions.choice: HTTP polling at 1–2s cadence with exponential backoff on idle.

alternatives.rejected_because: Requires ActionCable/AnyCable plumbing on the frontend; not viable in v2 timeline.

consequences: Polling cost is bounded and predictable.
No subscription transport in the schema.

open_questions.question: Do we need per-account daily generation budgets at launch?
open_questions.context: Bedrock spend is unbounded; cost rails imply some cap is desirable but the launch volume is uncertain.
open_questions.proposed_answer: Soft cap 50/day, hard cap 200/day per account.

modified.reason: Switched to HTTP polling per ADR-001.
